name: [BUG-GW-001] TC-GW-002 — CORS allow-origin must not be wildcard "*" on spr-fetcher routes
description:
**[KNOWN BUG — BUG-GW-001]**

**What this test does:**
Calls GET /spr-fetcher/api/v1/fetch/status and inspects CORS response headers.

**Expected:** access-control-allow-origin is set to a specific trusted origin
**Actual:** access-control-allow-origin: * (wildcard)

**Why this is dangerous on spr-fetcher specifically:**
Combined with BUG-SF-001 (no auth enforcement on the status endpoint), the wildcard CORS means any website can read the SPR Fetcher status — including the accountNumber and apiEndpoint — directly from a user's browser without the user knowing.

This creates a trivial CSRF-style data exfiltration path:
  1. User visits a malicious website
  2. Page scripts call GET /spr-fetcher/api/v1/fetch/status (no auth needed)
  3. CORS allows the response to be read by the malicious page
  4. accountNumber and apiEndpoint are silently exfiltrated
